items.title: Pope Leo XIV arrives to lead vigil on opening day of Youth Jubilee in Rome
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Pope Leo XIV thrilled hundreds of thousands of young Catholics on Saturday as he paraded through a vast field on the outskirts of Rome converted to host the Youth Jubilee.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: A half-million young Catholics arrive in Rome, awaiting Pope Leo XIV at Holy Year youth festival
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Hundreds of thousands of young Catholics poured into a vast field on Rome’s outskirts Saturday for the weekend highlight of the Vatican’s 2025 Holy Year: an evening vigil, outdoor slumber party and morning Mass celebrated by Pope Leo XIV that marks his first big encounter with the next generation of Catholics. (AP video shot by Paolo Santalucia)


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Israeli hostage families see no breakthrough after meeting with US envoy Witkoff
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Families of hostages expressed cautious satisfaction following their meeting with US envoy Steve Witkoff on Saturday but said there was nothing "new" in efforts to reach a deal for the release of the captives.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: Adam Sandler says the 'Happy Gilmore' golf swing might actually work
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Adam Sandler says the “Happy Gilmore” golf swing might look wild, but it’s fair game  — and even golf pros like Bryson DeChambeau are trying it out.
#adamsandler #happygilmore #happygilmore2 #shorts</article>

items.title: Palestinians find Gaza City and their homes in ruins on opportunity to return
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Palestinians who managed to return to their homes east of Gaza City found their neighborhood's unrecognizable, filled with rubble and destruction.

items.title: People mourn and bury those killed in Gaza since Friday
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Palestinians mourned some of the 44 people killed since Friday in northern Gaza, and gathered around those victims whose bodies arrived at Shifa hospital. (Production by Wafaa Shurafa)

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Egypt's strongman Ashraf Mahrous, also known by his nickname Kabonga pulled 20 cars on Friday during an attempt to create a new Guinness world record of most cars pulled by an individual. (AP Video shot by Ahmed Hatem)

items.title: From dawn to dusk, a starving Gaza family focuses on one thing: finding food
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Every morning, Abeer and Fadi Sobh wake up in their tent in the Gaza Strip to the same question: How will they find food for themselves and their six young children? (AP Production by Wafaa Shurafa)

items.title: Thousands of Palestinians walk from Zikim crossing as hunger-related deaths continue to mount
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A tidal wave of Palestinians walked back from the Zikim crossing, some of them carrying sacks of flour on their shoulders as hunger-related deaths continue to mount across the enclave, according to the Ministry of Health figures. (AP Production by Wafaa Shurafa)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: Thailand returns two wounded soldiers to Cambodia but holds 18 others
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Cambodia on Friday welcomed the return of two wounded soldiers who had been captured by the Thai army after the two sides had already implemented a ceasefire to end five days of combat over competing territorial claims. The soldiers were seen being carried on stretchers over the border checkpoint between Thailand’s Surin province and Cambodia’s Oddar Meanchey province before being put into the back of ambulances.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Republicans push to redraw districts in Texas to gain House seats
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Democratic state lawmakers tried to build a case Friday that Republicans in Texas have engineered a rushed and unfair process for redrawing the state's congressional districts in response to a push by President Donald Trump to win more GOP seats. 

#texas

items.title: Jenna Ortega says she doesn't let fame affect her like before: 'I'm not scared'
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>"Wednesday" star Jenna Ortega - who shot to fame when Tim Burton's hit show premiered in 2022 - explains how she's learning to deal with life in the spotlight. Season two of the supernatural high school adventure is released soon.
